channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IEBC Considers Phone Restrictions at Polling Stations to Safeguard Vote Secrecy in 2027 Elections
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has issued a strong warning to Kenyans against taking photos of ballot papers during the upcoming 2027 General Election. According to IEBC Co...

item.title: Christopher Nolan’s 'The Odyssey' Trailer Highlights Key Battle Scenes
item.description: Renowned filmmaker Christopher Nolan is all set to release his latest cinematic spectacle, 'The Odyssey', on 17th July. The newly launched trailer has generated significant excitement as it provides a...

item.title: Government Explains Delay in Paying Affordable Housing Interns
item.description: The Kenyan government has explained that delays in paying interns under the Affordable Housing Programme were caused by a payroll mix-up, rather than a shortage of funds. Speaking to NTV, Housing Prin...

item.title: Iran expands household subsidies as inflation accelerates
item.description: Iran’s government has acknowledged that inflation is rising sharply and is responding with broader household subsidies aimed at easing pressure on families. The move comes as the country faces a worse...

item.title: Explosion at Chinese Fireworks Factory Results in 21 Deaths and Numerous Injuries
item.description: A powerful explosion at a fireworks factory in Changsha, Hunan province, central China, has tragically claimed the lives of at least 21 people and left 61 others injured. The incident occurred at 4:40...

item.title: Top News Updates: Health, Reality TV, and Life After Matric
item.description: Sowetan’s AM Check-in highlights three important stories you should be aware of today. First, health authorities have urged the public not to panic over hantavirus fears. The national department of he...

item.title: Government scales up measures after over 500 hepatitis cases detected in Mwanza
item.description: Mwanza Region is facing a growing health concern as over 500 people have been diagnosed with hepatitis by April 2026. Health authorities report 523 cases of hepatitis B and 78 cases of hepatitis C. Th...

item.title: Assessing Whether the RBA Needs to Raise Interest Rates
item.description: The debate over whether the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) should increase interest rates remains divided among economists, policymakers, and the public. Some argue that higher interest rates are nec...

item.title: Key Questions Raised by the ‘Tayo Sa Wakas’ Trailer
item.description: ABS-CBN has recently released the trailer for 'Tayo Sa Wakas,' sparking curiosity among fans and movie enthusiasts. The trailer offers glimpses of the main characters portrayed by Donny Pangilinan and...

item.title: Mali junta leader Assimi Goita takes defence role amid escalating insurgent violence
item.description: Kiongozi wa serikali ya kijeshi nchini Mali, Assimi Goita, amechukua pia nafasi ya waziri wa ulinzi kufuatia kifo cha aliyekuwa waziri, Sadio Camara, aliyeuawa katika mashambulizi makubwa ya waasi wik...

item.title: OpenAI President Greg Brockman reveals $30B stake and links to Sam Altman amid Musk lawsuit
item.description: During the ongoing trial in California regarding OpenAI's conversion to a for-profit company, Greg Brockman, co-founder and president of OpenAI, disclosed in court that he holds nearly $30 billion wor...

item.title: US and Iran escalate tensions in Strait of Hormuz following naval incidents
item.description: Tensions between the United States and Iran have escalated sharply following claims by the US military that it sank six small Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. The US alleges that these boats w...

item.title: UDF Secures Overwhelming Victory in Kerala Assembly Elections, Reducing LDF to Minority
item.description: In a significant political development, the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) achieved a landslide victory in the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, winning 102 of 140 seats. This marks the alli...

item.title: Liberal Party expresses hope Robredo may run for president in 2028
item.description: The Liberal Party (LP) is expressing hope that former Vice President Leni Robredo may reconsider her earlier decision and run for president in the 2028 elections. LP president Erin Tanada emphasized t...

item.title: Ukraine to observe open-ended ceasefire starting 6 May in response to Russian Victory Day truce request
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky has declared an open-ended ceasefire set to begin at 12am on Wednesday, 6 May. This decision follows Russia’s request for a two-day truce on 8-9 May to mark Vict...

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni settle long-running legal dispute
item.description: Actors Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni have reached a settlement in their prolonged legal dispute, avoiding what would have been a costly civil trial. The conflict arose after Lively alleged that Bald...

item.title: Practical ways to use networking to secure employment opportunities
item.description: The article explains how networking can play a key role in helping people return to employment, especially after experiencing unemployment. It highlights that many job opportunities are not publicly a...

item.title: Woman Arrested After Fatal Dispute over Market Stall in Muhoroni
item.description: In Kisumu County, police have started investigations following a tragic incident in Muhoroni on Monday, May 4, where a woman lost her life after a dispute over a market stall. The victim, identified a...

item.title: Targeted killing of former JUI MPA Muhammad Idrees in Charsadda raises security concerns
item.description: In a tragic incident in Charsadda, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, renowned religious scholar and former JUI-F MPA Maulana Sheikh Muhammad Idrees was killed in a targeted attack. The incident occurred in Utmanzai...

item.title: Congress Candidate Motab Shaikh Wins Farakka Seat After Voter Restoration
item.description: In the recent West Bengal Assembly elections, Congress candidate Motab Shaikh emerged victorious in the Farakka constituency after his name was initially removed from the electoral rolls during the Sp...
